verdict_reason: Both Shankara and Ramanuja read 2.43 as supplying the predicative content of 2.42's relative clause; both insist the kāmya-karma is real and effective at its own level (svarga, bhoga, aiśvarya are real attainments) but bounded within saṃsāra. The engineering layer preserves both readings: 'the cycle works ... the bhoga-aiśvarya-gati is real' (matches Shankara's level-relativism and Ramanuja's preserved-validity). The constitutive force of kāmātman is not flattened to 'desirous'. The sectarian-relevant detail (Ramanuja's 'rituals performed as worship without fruit-attachment remain valid') is preserved in the source pack disagreements without being conflated with Shankara's level-relativism. The transliterated terms (kāmātman, svarga-para, kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulā, bhoga-aiśvarya-gati, saṃsāra, mokṣa) are all kept load-bearing. PASS for both 7.4 and 6.8.
